commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=156f29974147b77ad69b1c1674932b6d541ca0a3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Ollama's provider-policy `resolveThinkingProfile` so `reasoning: undefined` returns the full Ollama thinking profile and adds unit coverage for empty/undefined reasoning.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +11. Total +15 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, though I did not run a live Telegram/Ollama setup in this read-only review. Current main can call the Ollama profile resolver with `reasoning: undefined`, which returns off-only, while Ollama discovery maps `/api/show` `thinking` capability to `reasoning: true` when that metadata is available.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair job should run while the external proof gate is missing and maintainers still need to choose between this permissive fallback and the runtime-catalog sibling fix.

target.title: fix(ollama): treat undefined reasoning as potentially reasoning-capable
target.body:
## Summary

The `/think` command menu in Telegram shows only "default, off" for live-discovered Ollama models (e.g. `ollama/glm-5.2:cloud`) even though the model supports thinking and `/think medium` works fine. The root cause is in `resolveThinkingProfile` in `extensions/ollama/provider-policy-api.ts`: the condition `reasoning ? full : off-only` treats `undefined` reasoning (model not yet in catalog) the same as explicit `false`, returning only the off-only profile. This fix changes the condition to `reasoning !== false`, so only explicitly non-reasoning models are limited to off-only.

Fixes #93835

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Telegram `/think` menu displays only "default, off" for live-discovered Ollama models when the model's reasoning flag is undefined (not yet in catalog), even though models like `ollama/glm-5.2:cloud` support the full range of thinking levels.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 6.8.0-124-generic, Node v24, pnpm 11, OpenClaw main @ a37dd02

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test -- extensions/ollama/provider-policy-api.test.ts` then `pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts` then `pnpm test -- extensions/ollama/index.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**: provider-policy-api tests: 5 passed (including new undefined-reasoning case); thinking module tests: 53 passed; Ollama extension tests: 54 passed. `resolveThinkingProfile({})` and `resolveThinkingProfile({ reasoning: undefined })` now return `["off", "low", "medium", "high", "max"]` instead of `["off"]`.

**Observed result after the fix**: `resolveThinkingProfile` with undefined reasoning now returns the full thinking profile matching the behavior of explicitly reasoning-capable models. The `/think` menu will show all supported thinking levels for live-discovered Ollama models.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end Telegram bot scenario with a real Ollama instance and a dynamically discovered reasoning-capable model. Verified at unit level; all 112 related tests pass.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
pnpm test -- extensions/ollama/provider-policy-api.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)

pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  53 passed (53)

pnpm test -- extensions/ollama/index.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

### Integration / E2E

N/A — the change is a focused logic fix in the Ollama provider policy surface. The thinking profile resolution is covered by the existing test suites.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible — `reasoning: true` still returns full profile, `reasoning: false` still returns off-only
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations — all 112 related tests pass
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation — N/A, behavior change is self-documenting
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — no breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed
